meta.description: High-signal items only: contracts, earnings, funding, IPO signals, defense awards, acquisitions. urgency: immediate=act now, same_day=review today, watch=monitor

immediate.title: Space Force Mission Goes from Orders to Launch in Less than 17 Hours
immediate.summary: The Space Force started its second live Tactically Responsive Space mission June 19, working with a contractor to launch a satellite to low-Earth orbit in less than 17 hours. That spacecraft will now conduct a series of maneuver demonstrations with another vehicle. The post Space Force Mission Goes from Orders to Launch in Less than 17 Hours appeared first on Air & Space Forces Magazine .

immediate.title: Rocket Lab Launches Responsive Victus Haze Mission for Space Force
immediate.summary: Rocket lab launched a responsive space mission for the U.S. Space Force on Friday from New Zealand, as part of the Tactically Responsive Space (TacRS) Victus Haze mission. Space Systems [] The post Rocket Lab Launches Responsive Victus Haze Mission for Space Force appeared first on Via Satellite .

immediate.title: NASA Awards Solutions for Federal Enterprise Procurement Contracts
immediate.summary: NASA will begin processing the awards of multiple contracts for the Solutions for Enterprise‑wide Procurement (SEWP) VI Government-wide Acquisition Contract. The contract provides streamlined access to commercial products and services, including hardware, software, cloud services, cybersecurity tools, engineering and consulting services, and data intensive mission support capabilities. This competitive acquisition was conducted within three categories: []

immediate.title: Rocket Lab launches satellite for U.S. Space Force Victus Haze responsive space exercise
immediate.summary: The mission pairs Rocket Lab spacecraft with a True Anomaly vehicle in orbit to demonstrate rapid threat characterization and rendezvous operations The post Rocket Lab launches satellite for U.S. Space Force Victus Haze responsive space exercise appeared first on SpaceNews .

immediate.title: Tech Sergeant Promotion Rate Rises, But Pool of Eligibles Shrinks
immediate.summary: The Air Force’s promotion rate for new technical sergeants is holding steady in 2026 at around one-quarter of those eligible—but a sharp decline in the pool of those considered means the total number of new E-6s will be much lower. The post Tech Sergeant Promotion Rate Rises, But Pool of Eligibles Shrinks appeared first on Air & Space Forces Magazine .

immediate.title: New ‘Bridge’ Air Force One Arrives at Andrews for Commissioning Flights
immediate.summary: The Qatari-gifted aircraft that will serve as a “bridge” Air Force One will begin the commissioning flights needed to officially join the presidential airlift fleet, following its unveiling at Joint Base Andrews, Md., on June 19. The post New ‘Bridge’ Air Force One Arrives at Andrews for Commissioning Flights appeared first on Air & Space Forces Magazine .

immediate.title: A Faster Path to Orbit: How Hosted Payloads Are Changing Access to Space
immediate.summary: Historically, deploying a new capability in space required significant time, investment, and technical expertise. Companies often had little choice but to build nearly everything themselves—from spacecraft and payload integration to ground infrastructure and mission operations. Today, the space industry offers a different model: hosted payloads. “We’re seeing companies looking for the fastest, most economical way… The post A Faster Path to Orbit: How Hosted Payloads Are Changing Access to Space appeared first on Payload .

immediate.title: NASA to Cover US Spacewalk 95, Host Preview News Conference
immediate.summary: NASA astronauts will venture outside the International Space Station on Tuesday, June 30, to replace a wrist joint on the orbital complex’s Canadarm2 robotic arm. immediate.summary: Satellite servicing company Astroscale is working to transition from technology demonstrations to a regular series of missions, bolstered by a recent capital infusion. The post Astroscale raises funding to support growth strategy appeared first on SpaceNews .

immediate.title: New Report Looks at US Options for Potential Escalating Conflict in Space
immediate.summary: With Space Force leaders making the case that the service must grow to meet the demand of new threats, a recent workshop with 50 experts studied how those threats could impact American civilian and military capabilities as they escalate in severity and how the U.S. might respond to them. The post New Report Looks at US Options for Potential Escalating Conflict in Space appeared first on Air & Space Forces Magazine .

immediate.title: NASA's Roman Space Telescope arrives in Florida ahead of SpaceX Falcon Heavy launch this summer
immediate.summary: The Nancy Roman Grace Telescope has arrived at NASA's Kennedy Space Center for final preparations ahead of launching to join the orbital ranks of Hubble, Webb, Spitzer and Chandra.
